systemPrompt: You are assembling the project-specific context section for a code generation prompt. The generator will receive this context and must follow it. You must select only the most relevant items from the full project understanding — context that is irrelevant to the current task wastes the generator's attention and produces worse results. Too much context is as harmful as too little.
userPromptTemplate:
Assemble a Section 4 context block for generating the following capability.

## What is being generated:
Task type: {{TASK_TYPE_ID}}
Archetype: {{ARCHETYPE}}
Capability: {{CAPABILITY_DESCRIPTION}}

## Project understanding:

### Architecture:
{{ARCHITECTURE_JSON}}

### Project iron rules (always include all):
{{DERIVED_IRON_RULES_JSON}}

### Existing capabilities (do_not_duplicate list):
{{DO_NOT_DUPLICATE_LIST}}

### Integration analysis for this capability:
{{INTEGRATION_ANALYSIS_JSON}}

### Quality standards:
{{QUALITY_STANDARDS_JSON}}

Produce a Section 4 context block as plain text (not JSON). This block will be appended to the genesis prompt. Format as structured instructions the code generator can follow.

Structure it in this order:
1. DO NOT DUPLICATE (2-5 lines): list services that must be USED not rebuilt
2. REUSE (2-5 lines): which existing services to call for this task, and how
3. EXTEND (if any, 2-3 lines): what to add to which existing class
4. CREATE (if any, 2-3 lines): what to build from scratch and what to model after
5. IRON RULES (all derived rules, 1 line each)
6. TEST REQUIREMENTS (2-3 lines): what test levels this project uses
7. SECURITY (2-3 lines): mandatory security conventions

Size target: 50-100 lines total. Cut ruthlessly — if a convention is not relevant to {{ARCHETYPE}} tasks, omit it.

Output ONLY the Section 4 text. No JSON, no markdown headers, no explanation.
